label: p7_11_chain_match_post_fixes
description: Post-P5.5k + P-INFRA + P7.8/P7.11 re-run of the chain-position eigenvalue clustering test. Uses the trivial-irrep projected basis (TY → Z/3 filter, Schoen → Z/3×Z/3 filter) with modified Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization under L²(M) inner product followed by standard Hermitian EVP on the orthonormal basis. Eliminates the negative-eigenvalue / sign-flip pathology that caused the P6.3 retraction.

headlines.p6_3_retraction_status: PARTIALLY LIFTED — sign-flip pathology eliminated; both chains now unanimously favour Schoen across td=2..5
headlines.discrimination_verdict: Verdict (a)/(b) hybrid — Schoen wins on BOTH chains at every td, with TY's residual stable (rel-Δ ≤ 3% from td=3 onwards). Schoen's residual still drifts (rel-Δ 12-19%), trending DOWN with td (better fit at higher td). The original P6.3 headline 'TY +29.0 over Schoen on quark chain' was retracted for sign-flip; the post-P7.11 result is the OPPOSITE sign — Schoen consistently fits better, by roughly 9 nats on quark and 13-18 nats on lepton.
headlines.discriminating_channel: Chain match is now a usable channel — sign agreement across td=2..5 on BOTH chains is enough to support 'Schoen wins' qualitatively, even without strict 10% residual convergence on Schoen.
headlines.underlying_donaldson_caveat: Schoen's metric solve at max_iter=100, donaldson_tol=1e-6 lands at sigma=1.85 — about 7× TY's 0.27. The Schoen Donaldson noise floor is the dominant driver of Schoen's residual drift; the chain-match comparator still favours Schoen even with this disadvantage. Tightening Schoen's metric (more Adam steps post-Donaldson) is expected to bring Schoen's residual to a tighter envelope.
